message.content: Tool-calling agents should avoid repeated tool loops by implementing strict input deduplication and state-aware context management. First, the agent must check if the exact same tool and arguments have been recently executed. If a call yields no new information or results in a self-referential error, the agent should halt further identical attempts. Second, incorporate a maximum iteration limit to prevent infinite cycles. Third, enhance prompt engineering to encourage reflection; instruct the agent to evaluate whether previous attempts were sufficient before retrying. Finally, utilize structured error handling where specific failure modes trigger alternative strategies rather than retries. By combining these techniques—deduplication, iteration caps, reflective prompting, and strategic error handling—agents can break repetitive patterns, conserve computational resources, and ensure progress toward task completion.
